body_txt: This character tends to have a Limited Social Circle , or sometimes even just an Only Friend . While they may have a crush, any romance tends to develop gradually and naturally rather than relying on charm, looks, or social status. This character isn't known for being particularly athletic, popular, or socially dominant. They might be into hobbies like video games or other niche interests, rather than sports or other traditionally "cool" activities. While they're usually intelligent, they often face challenges in school and social situations, whether they're Book Dumb or not. This archetype often deals with everyday conflicts — bullying, navigating friendships, and minor family issues. Their home life usually includes at least one sibling, adding a relatable dynamic to their story. As they grow older, particularly into high school or young adulthood, they may face milestones like learning to drive, getting their first job, or exploring romantic relationships. The Awkward Relatable Schoolkid's stories tend to focus more on self-acceptance, resilience, and learning to appreciate those who care about them, rather than major triumphs or victories. They resonate with audiences because they're not a standout star — they're just an average kid trying to figure things out. This character is relatable because they're familiar. Unlike the Big Man on Campus , All American Boy , Alpha Bitch , etc., they're not a standout star, but rather likable in their own way. Their stories are less about big victories and more about self-acceptance, resilience, and appreciating those who care about them. This trope has become increasingly popular in children's media, as audiences gravitate toward more grounded, relatable characters who don't rely on being exceptional to feel significant. See The Everyman for his adult counterpart. Compare Normal People , The Generic Guy , Vanilla Protagonist , Unlucky Everydude , Ordinary High-School Student , and Ridiculously Average Guy . A Spider-Man Send-Up is often one in their secret identity. Female examples do exist but are harder to come by, possibly because Most Writers Are Male . Female examples often overlap with Stock Shoujo Heroine . Contrast Stock '90s Bad Boy , an irreverent and mischievous youth who wants to be seen as cool.
